texto: ...we steal with our eyes closed to the conditions in which the poor, who make our affluence possible, live. We covet what our neighbours have and want more of the same.
explicacao: Citação de Karen Baker-Fletcher sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. “...we steal with our eyes closed to the conditions in which the poor, who make our affluence poss…” articula vida, affluence, greed em registro de vida, com relevância para contextos como greed e poor.

frase_original: ...we steal with our eyes closed to the conditions in which the poor, who make our affluence possible, live. We covet what our neighbours have and want more of the same.

texto: Neoclassical theology corrects misconceptions about God that are neither experientially true nor biblically grounded. If God were "omnipotent" in the sense of absolute determinism, then creation, and especially humans, would have no freedom. Freedom and absolute determinism negate one another. From the perspective of process metaphysics, if God were fully deterministic, then one could not speak of freedom of the will, the ability to choose to participate in God's creativity or not. God is in control, but God is not a control freak! God is not a "tyrant," puppeter, or robotic engineer!
explicacao: Citação de Karen Baker-Fletcher sobre temas ligados a fe. “Neoclassical theology corrects misconceptions about God that are neither experientially true nor …” articula fe, control, freedom em registro de fe, com relevância para contextos como freedom e god.

From the perspective of process metaphysics, if God were fully deterministic, then one could not speak of freedom of the will, the ability to choose to participate in God's creativity or not.
